Job Post Details

Senior Denodo Developer – Data Integration - job post

General Dynamics Mission Systems, Inc
(part of General Dynamics)
3.63.6 out of 5 stars
Remote
$132,930 - $143,835 a year - Full-time

Job details

Here’s how the job details align with your profile.

Pay

Job type

&nbsp;

Full job description

Basic Qualifications :
Bachelor’s degree in Information Technology, Engineering and/or related field or equivalent experience, plus 8 years of related experience or Master’s degree and 6 years of related experience.

Due to the nature of work performed within our facilities, U.S. citizenship is required.

Responsibilities for this Position:
General Dynamics Mission Systems has an immediate opening for a Sr Advanced Denodo Developer – Data Integration. This position provides an opportunity to further advance the cutting-edge technology that supports some of our nation’s core defense/intelligence services and systems. General Dynamics Mission Systems employees work closely with esteemed customers to develop solutions that allow them to carry out high-stakes national security missions.

A Denodo Developer designs, develops, and maintains data virtualization solutions using the Denodo Platform, integrating data from multiple sources to deliver unified, efficient business data views.

Core Responsibilities
  • Develop and maintain Denodo Views and data virtualization layers to unify data from diverse sources such as relational databases, cloud platforms, big data, and SaaS applications
  • Optimize query performance and tune the Denodo environment to ensure efficient data retrieval
  • Design and implement scheduling jobs within Denodo (e.g., Denodo Scheduler) to automate data workflows
  • Coordinate with business stakeholders, data architects, and analysts to gather requirements, analyze data needs, and design appropriate virtual data models
  • Develop new functionalities and maintain existing features on the Denodo platform, including writing automated tests and maintaining product documentation
  • Implement security protocols and access controls to protect data within the virtualization platform
  • Troubleshoot integration and data quality issues within the virtualized environment
Required Skills and Qualifications
  • Proficiency with Denodo Platform, especially in developing data views and working within its virtualization environment
  • Strong knowledge of SQL and relational database design; ability to write complex queries and perform data modeling
  • Experience with Java programming and object-oriented design principles
  • Familiarity with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud), container technologies (Docker, Kubernetes), and web technologies is advantageous
  • Strong analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills to collaborate effectively across teams and translate technical requirements
  • Experience with tools like Git, Jenkins, Maven, and familiarity with Agile software development methodologies
  • Certifications such as Denodo Platform Certified Developer, Administrator, or Architect are highly valued
  • Preferred experience includes working knowledge of web services (REST APIs), security protocols (OAuth, SAML), Snowflake Platform and integration with BI tools like Tableau or Power BI
  • Typically requires 5-7 years of experience in software development or specifically working with data virtualization tools
  • Prior experience with ETL tools (Informatica, DataStage) and data quality frameworks can be beneficial but not mandatory
Salary Note: This estimate represents the typical salary range for this position based on experience and other factors (geographic location, etc.). Actual pay may vary. This job posting will remain open until the position is filled. Combined Salary Range: USD $132,930.00 - USD $143,835.00 /Yr. Company Overview:
General Dynamics Mission Systems (GDMS) engineers a diverse portfolio of high technology solutions, products and services that enable customers to successfully execute missions across all domains of operation. With a global team of 12,000+ top professionals, we partner with the best in industry to expand the bounds of innovation in the defense and scientific arenas. Given the nature of our work and who we are, we value trust, honesty, alignment and transparency. We offer highly competitive benefits and pride ourselves in being a great place to work with a shared sense of purpose. You will also enjoy a flexible work environment where contributions are recognized and rewarded. If who we are and what we do resonates with you, we invite you to join our high-performance team!

Equal Opportunity Employer / Individuals with Disabilities / Protected Veterans
